skill.name: competitor-teardown
skill.description: Structured competitive analysis skill that guides users through 7-layer analysis framework. Includes feature matrices, SWOT analysis, positioning maps, pricing comparison, review mining, and UX screenshots. Uses inference.sh CLI for research and web scraping.
skill.summary: Structured competitive analysis with feature matrices, SWOT, positioning maps, and UX review using the inference.sh CLI tool.
skill.author: inference-sh-9

security_audit.summary: This skill is a legitimate competitive analysis framework. All static findings are false positives: the external_commands are example bash commands in documentation (not actual execution), network URLs are legitimate tool endpoints, and the 'SAML' string was misidentified as 'SAM database'. No malicious behavior detected.

critical_findings.title: Pipe to Shell Pattern
critical_findings.description: The skill documents a curl|sh installation pattern for inference.sh CLI. This is a standard installation method used by many legitimate tools (Docker, npm, etc.), not malicious code.
critical_findings.confidence_reasoning: The 'curl | sh' pattern is standard industry practice for CLI tool installation. This is documentation, not code execution.

critical_findings.title: Windows SAM Database Detection
critical_findings.description: Scanner detected 'SAM' string at line 115. This is actually 'SAML' (Security Assertion Markup Language), a standard SSO protocol, not the Windows SAM database.
critical_findings.confidence_reasoning: The text 'SSO/SAML' in the feature matrix refers to SAML authentication, a standard enterprise feature. Scanner misidentified the substring.

medium_findings.title: Shell Command Examples in Documentation
medium_findings.description: Skill contains multiple bash command examples using infsh CLI. These are documentation/examples, not executable code. The skill is a competitive analysis guide, not a script.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: These are example commands shown to users in code blocks for competitive research. They are not executed by the skill itself.

low_findings.title: Network URLs in Documentation
low_findings.description: Skill contains URLs to inference.sh services and example competitor websites. These are legitimate tool endpoints and placeholder URLs for competitive analysis.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: URLs are legitimate service endpoints for the inference.sh CLI tool being documented and example placeholder domains.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Commands
low_findings.description: Skill includes commands to search for competitor information, funding, reviews, and news. This is expected behavior for a competitive analysis skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Competitive research inherently requires searching for competitor data. This is the purpose of the skill, not malicious reconnaissance.

content.value_statement: This skill provides a structured framework for competitive analysis, guiding users through feature comparisons, SWOT analysis, pricing benchmarks, and market positioning to inform product strategy.
actual_capabilities: Guide users through 7-layer competitive analysis framework
Generate feature matrices comparing products across multiple dimensions
Create SWOT analysis templates for each competitor
Build positioning maps with customizable axes
Extract and analyze competitor pricing from web sources
Mine user reviews from platforms like G2, Capterra, and Reddit
limitations: Requires inference.sh CLI tools to be installed for automated research
Does not provide real-time data; research commands return snapshot results
Visual generation depends on Python matplotlib availability
Cannot access password-protected competitor resources or private data

use_cases.title: Product Manager Market Analysis
use_cases.description: Product managers use this skill to understand competitive landscape, identify market gaps, and inform product roadmap decisions.

use_cases.title: Sales Team Competitive Positioning
use_cases.description: Sales teams use competitive intelligence to position their solution against rivals, understand competitor weaknesses, and handle objection handling.
use_cases.target_user: Sales representatives and sales enablement teams

use_cases.title: Investor Due Diligence
use_cases.description: Investors use this framework to quickly understand market dynamics, competitive moats, and investment thesis for portfolio companies.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Competitor Overview
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the competitor-teardown skill to analyze [Competitor Name]. Start with the 7-layer framework and provide a brief overview of their product, pricing, and market positioning.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick competitive scan for a single competitor

prompt_templates.title: Feature Matrix Comparison
prompt_templates.prompt: Using the competitor-teardown skill, create a feature matrix comparing our product [Your Product] against [Competitor A], [Competitor B], and [Competitor C]. Include at least 8 key features.

prompt_templates.title: Full Market Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Conduct a full competitive landscape analysis using the competitor-teardown skill. Identify the top 5 competitors in [Market Category], analyze their strengths and weaknesses using SWOT, and create a positioning map.
prompt_templates.scenario: Comprehensive market analysis with multiple competitors

prompt_templates.title: Pricing Strategy Research
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the competitor-teardown skill to research pricing for [Competitor A], [Competitor B], and [Competitor C]. Create a pricing comparison table and identify hidden costs or pricing strategies.
prompt_templates.scenario: Competitive pricing analysis for pricing strategy

output_examples.input: Analyze Asana vs Monday.com for project management
output_examples.output:
## Competitive Analysis: Asana vs Monday.com

### Feature Matrix
| Feature | Our Product | Asana | Monday.com |
|---------|:---:|:---:|:---:|
| Templates | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Timeline | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Automations | ✅ | Limited | ✅ |
| Reporting | Advanced | Basic | Advanced |

### SWOT Analysis - Asana
| Strengths | Weaknesses |
|-----------|------------|
| Strong brand recognition | Higher price point |
| Extensive integrations | Complex for small teams |

### Positioning
[2x2 matrix showing Monday.com as simple/SMB, Asana as complex/enterprise]

## Pricing Comparison

| Plan | Our Product | Competitor A | Competitor B |
|------|:---:|:---:|:---:|
| Free | 5 users | 3 users | No |
| Starter | $12 | $15 | $10 |
| Pro | $25 | $30 | $24 |

### Hidden Costs Identified
- Competitor A: $500 setup fee
- Competitor B: Metered API charges

best_practices: Lead with features where your product has clear advantages, but be honest about competitor strengths to maintain credibility
Date all research findings and set quarterly reminders to refresh competitive intelligence
Focus analysis on top 3-5 direct competitors to avoid analysis paralysis
anti_patterns: Biased analysis that ignores competitor strengths damages credibility with stakeholders
Outdated data leads to wrong strategic conclusions - always verify recency
Feature-only comparison misses critical dimensions like positioning, pricing, and market traction

faq.question: What tools does this skill use for research?
faq.answer: This skill uses inference.sh CLI tools including tavily for web search, exa for AI-powered search, and browser agents for screenshots.

faq.question: How current is the competitive data?
faq.answer: Data freshness depends on when you run the research commands. The skill provides a framework but does not include built-in data - you run commands to fetch current information.

faq.question: Can I analyze private or password-protected competitor sites?
faq.answer: No. The skill can only analyze publicly accessible information. For proprietary competitor research, use other methods like customer interviews or industry reports.

faq.question: How many competitors should I analyze at once?
faq.answer: Focus on the top 3-5 direct competitors for depth. You can mention additional competitors briefly, but detailed analysis of too many competitors leads to analysis paralysis.

faq.question: Does this skill generate visuals automatically?
faq.answer: The skill provides templates and can run Python code to generate positioning maps, but you need matplotlib installed for visual generation.

faq.question: Can I use this for investor presentations?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill includes executive summary templates and deliverable formats suitable for investor decks and board presentations.
